lines.text: (off the phone) [warmly] Grab a seat, I'll be right with you.
(then, into the phone) No, that's the county's. [pause] The culvert out on the section line, that's not us. Tell him to call Roads.
(then) Yeah. The number's on their website somewhere. [pause] Alright. Bye.
[flatly] They reassessed the home place. I came about the sixty days.
Forty-two oh six, due the fifteenth. [pause] Okay.
(then) And you're sitting at, what, [pause] twenty-one hundred in the checking.
[flatly] Twenty-one forty. As of the tenth.
Right.
There's a hardship program. [pause] There was. Let me just...
(then) It's paused. [hesitates] Not paused — there's no — there isn't a mechanism right now. Is what that means.
[flatly] Who do I talk to, then.
That's the thing. It went federal in the spring. [pause] There's no office to send you to anymore, it just says pending review.
That's — [pause] hang on. You've got a payroll line on here.
(reading) [curious] NASA. Payroll. Zero dollars, quarterly, going back to seventy-two.
(then) And it's got you down here as astronaut. Active. [pause] I've never seen the system do that.
(then) I can scrub it for you while we're in here. It's just sitting there wrong anyway.
(quick) [flatly] Leave it.
[quietly] It's been there fifty years. It can stay.
Okay.
Our scanner's been down since Tuesday. [pause] So if your paperwork came in today and I couldn't scan it, it wouldn't post until next cycle.
That buys you about sixty days. [quietly] Don't tell my branch manager.
[flatly] What do I owe you.
It's a bank, Mr. Dawes. [pause] You owe us four thousand dollars.
[flatly] That culvert. On the section line.
[hesitates] ...Yeah?
Route 12, out by the Hollen place. [pause] Log chain and a tractor'll do it. Hour of work.
...I'll pass that on. [pause] Every quarter, huh.
[tired] "Working for communities like yours."
